name: Electrical Power Grid
tagline: Synchronised generation, transmission, and distribution that move gigawatts across continents with second-level control
problem:
Local dynamos can light one mill, but civilisation wanted power where rivers were absent. The grid problem is moving electrical energy with acceptable loss, preserving voltage for end users, and keeping thousands of generators phase-locked so clocks stay honest and motors do not fight each other.

Faults propagate at light speed in conductors; trees touch lines; storms trip relays. Without protection coordination, a neighbourhood short can black out a province. Stability is a coupled control problem: mechanical inertia, excitation systems, tap changers, and human dispatchers trading frequency against fuel cost.

The grid is therefore not wires alone but standards: frequency, earthing practices, harmonic limits, and markets that incentivise reserves.
overview:
Generators produce three-phase sinusoidal voltages stepped up by transformers for long-distance transmission at hundreds of kilovolts. Bundle conductors reduce corona; series compensation and FACTS devices manage reactive power and oscillations. Substations break and reconfigure paths; distribution feeders step down to customer voltage classes.

Protection uses relays measuring current differentials, distance, and frequency-rate-of-change. SCADA overlays human visibility; automatic generation control nudges power setpoints to restore scheduled interchanges. Inverter-based resources (solar, batteries, wind) change classical inertia assumptions — new grid-forming controls emulate stability services.

Earthing practices manage touch potentials during faults. Insulation coordination selects BIL levels for lightning. Underground urban networks trade capacitance and cost against aesthetics.

Operationally, the grid is one of civilisation’s largest real-time control loops — imperfect, political, and indispensable.

principles.name: AC versus DC trade-offs
principles.explanation: Transformers make AC trivial to step in voltage; HVDC wins on very long submarine links and asynchronous interties where phase is incompatible.

principles.name: Reactive power
principles.explanation: Inductive loads need vars; capacitors, reactors, and generator excitation supply them — neglecting vars collapses voltage locally.

principles.name: Symmetrical components
principles.explanation: Unbalanced faults decompose into positive, negative, and zero sequences — the algebra protection engineers live in.

principles.name: Stability margin
principles.explanation: Rotor angle swings after a fault must decay; insufficient synchronising torque leads to pole slipping and separation.

components.name: Transmission lines and towers
components.function: Carries bulk power with minimal I²R loss
components.madeFrom: ACSR conductors, steel lattice, porcelain or composite insulators

components.name: Power transformers
components.function: Steps voltage between transmission, sub-transmission, and distribution
components.madeFrom: Laminated core, copper windings, mineral or ester oil
components.criticalNote: Buchholz relays detect internal arcing gas

components.name: Protective relays and IEDs
components.function: Detects faults and trips breakers selectively
components.madeFrom: Microprocessor relays with fibre time sync

components.name: SCADA and EMS
components.function: Supervises topology, alarms, and economic dispatch

rawMaterials.name: Transformer core steel
earthLocations: Speciality rolling mills in Japan, USA, EU

rawMaterials.name: Insulating oil or gas
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Dry-type transformers trade size for maintenance

buildSteps.title: Load-flow and short-circuit studies
buildSteps.description: Model N-1 contingencies; verify thermal ratings; coordinate relay curves.
buildSteps.warningNote: Underestimating motor starting current causes nuisance trips

buildSteps.title: Erect structures and string conductors
buildSteps.description: Maintain sags and clearances; install armour grounding; anti-climb where public access exists.
buildSteps.warningNote: Induction from parallel lines can energise de-energised spans — always verify

buildSteps.title: Commission substations
buildSteps.description: Apply vacuum to transformers; measure partial discharge; phasing checks before closing rings.
buildSteps.warningNote: Incorrect polarity on VTs mis-aims relays

buildSteps.title: Protective relay testing
buildSteps.description: Secondary inject currents and voltages; verify trip times; document settings versions.
buildSteps.warningNote: Firmware updates invalidate old setting sheets

buildSteps.title: Energise and monitor stability
buildSteps.description: Start with radial feeds; close loops after phase checks; tune AGC and damping controllers.

history.event: Great Barrington demonstration transmits AC power — early argument for polyphase systems.
Niagara Falls Adams Plant sends long-distance AC transmission to Buffalo.
HVDC link connects Gotland — submarine DC interties mature.

inventors.name: Nikola Tesla
inventors.contribution: Polyphase AC systems enabling practical long-distance transmission

impact:
Grids synchronised industry to a clock of electrons — enabling motors, lighting, and later computing at arbitrary distance from fuel piles. They also created blackout risk as a political fact.

Off-world, microgrids precede continents of copper; the same physics applies at smaller perimeters.

videos.title: Power system basics
videos.description: Accredited electrical engineering introductions to load flow and protection zones.
